title: Major Health Insurance Company Pays $342 Million Following Billing Investigation
subtitle: The government successfully recovers large sums from a Medicare Advantage provider after years of concerns over overcharging.

summary.en: Elevance Health has paid the U.S. government $342 million to settle claims of overcharging the Medicare Advantage program. This payment follows a government warning about persistent billing errors and a failure to follow federal regulations. Experts view the move as a significant step toward holding large health insurance companies accountable. Medicare Advantage plans are popular with millions of Americans because they offer extra benefits like dental and hearing coverage. However, the industry continues to face debates regarding whether these private plans are fair to taxpayers.
summary.zh: 艾利凡斯健康已向美國政府支付三點四二億美元，以和解關於其向聯邦醫療保險優勢計畫超額收費的指控。此付款是在政府針對持續性的帳單錯誤及未遵守聯邦法規發出警告後所進行的。專家認為此舉是讓大型健康保險公司負起責任的重要一步。聯邦醫療保險優勢計畫因提供牙科與聽力等額外福利，受到數百萬美國人歡迎。然而，該產業仍面臨關於這些私人計畫對納稅人是否公平的爭論。

paragraphs.en: A major health insurance company, Elevance Health, has paid the U.S. government more than $342 million. This large payment comes after the company faced serious claims that it overcharged the federal healthcare program for several years. According to court records, the money was sent to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) on May 27. Government lawyers later confirmed the payment in a court filing on June 22.
paragraphs.zh: 大型健康保險公司「艾利凡斯健康 (Elevance Health)」已向美國政府支付超過三點四二億美元。這筆鉅額款項是在該公司面臨多年來向聯邦醫療保健計畫超額收費的嚴重指控後所支付的。根據法院紀錄，這筆款項已於五月二十七日匯給聯邦醫療保險與醫療補助服務中心 (CMS)。政府律師隨後在六月二十二日的法庭文件中證實了這筆付款。

paragraphs.en: This development is an update to the ongoing efforts by the government to fix billing problems within the Medicare Advantage system. Medicare Advantage plans are private insurance options that over 35 million Americans use. These plans are popular because they often provide extra benefits, such as hearing aids and dental coverage, which traditional Medicare does not include. For many patients, these plans can be a cheaper alternative to buying extra insurance to cover gaps in their healthcare.
paragraphs.zh: 此進展是政府為修正聯邦醫療保險優勢計畫 (Medicare Advantage) 系統內帳單問題所持續努力的一部分。聯邦醫療保險優勢計畫是超過三千五百萬名美國人使用的私人保險選項。這些計畫之所以受歡迎，是因為它們通常提供傳統聯邦醫療保險所不包含的額外福利，例如助聽器和牙科保險。對許多病患而言，這些計畫是購買額外保險以填補醫療保障缺口的較便宜替代方案。

paragraphs.en: However, the system has faced criticism for a long time. The government has been investigating claims that some health plans exaggerate how sick their patients are to receive higher payments. Medicare pays insurance companies more money to care for sicker patients, but the rules require that these conditions must be clearly documented in medical records. Researchers have found that medical coding mistakes often lead to bills that are much higher than they should be, costing taxpayers billions of dollars every year.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，該系統長期以來一直面臨批評。政府一直在調查相關指控，稱部分健康保險計畫誇大病患的病情，以獲取更高的給付。聯邦醫療保險會支付保險公司更多費用以照護病情較重的病患，但規定要求這些病況必須在醫療紀錄中清楚記載。研究人員發現，醫療編碼錯誤常導致帳單金額遠高於應有水準，每年讓納稅人損失數十億美元。

paragraphs.en: The recent payment from Elevance Health followed a strong warning from the CMS in February. At that time, the agency threatened to stop the company from enrolling new members unless it fixed what officials called “substantial and persistent noncompliance.” This means the company was not following federal rules that require health plans to provide accurate billing data and return any extra money they received by mistake. In an email to CMS staff, Elevance described the $342 million as a return of the total overpayment amount that government auditors had identified.
paragraphs.zh: 艾利凡斯健康近期支付的款項，源於聯邦醫療保險與醫療補助服務中心在二月份發出的強烈警告。當時，該機構威脅若該公司不修正官員所謂「重大且持續性的違規行為」，將禁止其招收新會員。這意味著該公司未遵守聯邦規定，即健康保險計畫必須提供準確的帳單數據，並退還錯誤收取的額外款項。艾利凡斯在給該中心員工的電子郵件中，將這三點四二億美元描述為退還政府審計人員所認定之超額給付總額。

paragraphs.en: Industry experts are watching this situation closely. David Lipschutz, an attorney with the Center for Medicare Advocacy, noted that he had never seen a case like this before. He explained that health plans usually try to delay paying back money for many years, often using legal processes to avoid immediate action. David Meyers, an associate professor at the Brown University School of Public Health, called the payment a significant step in the right direction. He noted that it is a big win for the government to successfully recover such a large amount of money from a major company.
paragraphs.zh: 產業專家正密切關注此情況。聯邦醫療保險倡導中心 (Center for Medicare Advocacy) 的律師大衛·利普舒茨 (David Lipschutz) 指出，他從未見過類似案例。他解釋說，健康保險計畫通常會試圖拖延還款多年，並常利用法律程序來規避立即行動。布朗大學公共衛生學院副教授大衛·梅耶斯 (David Meyers) 稱此付款是朝正確方向邁出的重要一步。他表示，政府能成功從一家大型企業追回如此鉅額款項，是一項重大勝利。

paragraphs.en: Despite this payment, the debate over Medicare Advantage remains active. Whistleblower lawsuits—legal cases brought by people who report wrongdoing within their own companies—have been the main way the government has tried to recover money in the past. For example, in January, Kaiser Permanente agreed to pay $556 million to settle claims that it billed the government for medical conditions that patients did not actually have. The industry generally disputes these claims, arguing that their billing practices are complex but legal.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管已支付這筆款項，關於聯邦醫療保險優勢計畫的爭論依然激烈。吹哨者訴訟——即由舉報公司內部不法行為的人士所提起的法律案件——是過去政府嘗試追回款項的主要方式。例如，凱薩醫療機構 (Kaiser Permanente) 在一月份同意支付五點五六億美元，以和解其被控向政府申報病患實際上並未罹患之疾病的指控。保險業普遍對這些指控提出異議，主張其帳單作業雖複雜但均屬合法。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the relationship between health insurance companies and the government remains complicated. A spokesperson for Elevance Health, Leslie Porras, stated that the company continues to have a constructive dialogue with the CMS. She added that the company remains optimistic that they can reach a full resolution and values its long-standing relationship with the agency. As the government continues its crackdown on billing errors, the focus will likely remain on whether these new enforcement actions will lead to more accurate billing across the entire insurance industry.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，健康保險公司與政府之間的關係依然複雜。艾利凡斯健康的發言人萊斯利·波拉斯 (Leslie Porras) 表示，該公司持續與聯邦醫療保險與醫療補助服務中心進行建設性對話。她補充說，公司對能達成全面解決方案保持樂觀，並重視與該機構的長期合作關係。隨著政府持續打擊帳單錯誤，焦點可能會持續放在這些新的執法行動，是否能促使整個保險業的帳單作業更加準確。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To claim that something is greater or more serious than it really is.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞在商業語境中常被誤解為單純的「強調」，但在此處指為了獲利而虛報病情嚴重程度。文中：The government has been investigating claims that some health plans exaggerate how sick their patients are to receive higher payments.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To reach an official agreement to end a legal argument or dispute.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「定居」或「解決問題」，在此法律語境下特指雙方達成協議以結束訴訟。文中：For example, in January, Kaiser Permanente agreed to pay $556 million to settle claims that it billed the government for medical conditions that patients did not actually have.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Severe measures to restrict or discourage undesirable or illegal people or activities.
keyTerms.noteZh: 由動詞片語 crack down 演變而來的名詞，常指政府或權威機構採取強硬手段整頓。文中：As the government continues its crackdown on billing errors, the focus will likely remain on whether these new enforcement actions will lead to more accurate billing across the entire insurance industry.

sources.name: KFF Health News
sources.title: Medicare Advantage Company Pays $342M to Government in Midst of Billing Probe

previousArticles.title: Major Health Insurance Company Pays Millions Over Billing Mistakes
previousArticles.summaryEn: A large health insurance company, Elevance Health, has paid the U.S. government $342 million to settle claims of overcharging. This payment follows a government warning about persistent billing errors in its Medicare Advantage plans. The action is part of a wider effort to address billions of dollars in suspected fraud across the healthcare industry. Experts view this as a positive step toward holding private insurance companies accountable. It comes shortly after a massive, separate investigation charged hundreds of people for healthcare fraud.

quiz.question: What specific action did the CMS threaten to take against Elevance Health in February?

options.A: To sue the company for billions of dollars in damages.
options.B: To prevent the company from signing up new insurance members.
options.C: To terminate the company's existing contracts with all patients.
options.D: To force the company to pay an additional $556 million fine.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第四段，CMS 在二月曾威脅若 Elevance Health 不修正其「持續且重大的違規行為」，將禁止該公司招募新會員。選項 A 錯誤，文章未提到起訴金額為數十億；選項 C 錯誤，文中並未提及終止現有合約；選項 D 錯誤，5.56 億元是 Kaiser Permanente 的和解金，並非 Elevance 的罰款。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why is the payment from Elevance Health considered unusual by industry experts?

options.A: Because the amount paid was significantly lower than what auditors expected.
options.B: Because the company admitted to criminal wrongdoing in court records.
options.C: Because the company agreed to pay without engaging in a long-term legal delay.
options.D: Because the payment was made directly to patients instead of the government.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第五段，專家 David Lipschutz 指出，保險公司通常會利用法律程序拖延數年以避免立即還款，而 Elevance 此次直接還款的行為顯得不同尋常。選項 A 錯誤，文中未指明金額低於預期；選項 B 錯誤，文中未提到公司承認刑事罪行；選項 D 錯誤，款項是支付給 CMS（政府機構），而非患者。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'constructive' mean in the context of the company's dialogue with the CMS?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在最後一段中，Elevance 的發言人表示公司與 CMS 保持「constructive dialogue」，意指雙方進行的是有建設性、能解決問題的溝通。選項 B 錯誤，constructive 強調的是成果而非形式；選項 C 錯誤，雖然關係複雜，但該詞本身帶有正面意涵；選項 D 錯誤，這與發言人表達的樂觀態度與和解意願不符。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding Medicare Advantage?

options.A: The government is planning to replace private insurance with traditional Medicare.
options.B: Elevance Health is the only company currently under investigation for billing errors.
options.C: Whistleblower lawsuits are the only effective way to recover overpaid tax money.
options.D: The government is increasing enforcement to address persistent billing inaccuracies in private plans.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章整體旨在探討政府如何透過執法行動（如 Elevance 的還款案）來解決 Medicare Advantage 系統中長期存在的帳單錯誤與過度收費問題。選項 A 錯誤，文中未提及要廢除私人保險；選項 B 錯誤，文中提到了 Kaiser Permanente 等其他案例；選項 C 錯誤，文中僅提到吹哨者訴訟是過去的主要方式，並非「唯一」方式。
